prompt: Create a full financial business case for ProjectNova, a $3M digital transformation project replacing legacy order management with a cloud-native platform. 18-month implementation, 5-year investment horizon, 150-person user base.
assertions: produces a benefit register with ≥8 quantified benefits across revenue, cost reduction, and productivity categories
every benefit has an assigned confidence level (High / Medium / Low)
TCO model covers implementation costs, ongoing operational costs, transition costs, and opportunity costs
year-by-year cash flow table spans the full 5-year investment horizon
calculates NPV, IRR, payback period, and benefit-cost ratio
presents conservative, expected, and optimistic scenarios for all financial metrics
includes sensitivity analysis on ≥3 key assumptions with tornado diagram or table
calculates cost of delay per month
evidence tags present on every major claim — not solely [SUPUESTO]
output file named {fase}_business_case_ProjectNova_{WIP}.md

prompt: Build a business case comparing 3 vendor options for a CRM platform: Salesforce, HubSpot, and a custom-built solution. Annual revenue $50M, 80-person sales team, 3-year horizon.
assertions: includes do-nothing/status-quo as a mandatory baseline option alongside the 3 vendors
produces per-option TCO models with consistent scope boundaries and discount rate
side-by-side comparison matrix includes ≥6 weighted criteria (financial + strategic + operational)
every qualitative score has written justification — no unexplained ratings
weights are explicitly stated and sum to 100%
financial metrics (NPV, payback) calculated independently for each option
trade-off analysis explicitly states what is gained and sacrificed per option
addresses vendor lock-in risk and switching cost for each option
includes a recommendation with conditions under which it would change

prompt: Create a business case for ProjectShield, a regulatory compliance project mandated by new data privacy legislation. $1.2M budget, no direct revenue benefit. Non-compliance penalty is $5M per incident.
assertions: recognizes compliance as a risk avoidance / cost avoidance benefit category
quantifies benefit as expected penalty avoidance (probability × penalty magnitude)
includes reputational damage and customer trust as intangible benefits with proxy metrics
does not fabricate direct revenue benefits where none exist
presents cost of non-compliance scenario vs cost of compliance scenario
TCO model includes ongoing compliance monitoring and audit costs beyond initial implementation
NPV calculation reflects risk-adjusted benefit (not full $5M penalty as guaranteed saving)
recommends approval framing appropriate for mandatory/regulatory projects

prompt: Our ProjectOrbit business case was approved at $2M but implementation costs have increased by 40% due to integration complexity. Revise the business case with the new $2.8M cost baseline.
assertions: recalculates all financial metrics (NPV, IRR, payback, BCR) with the updated $2.8M cost baseline
shows before-and-after comparison of key metrics to quantify the impact
identifies which metrics cross from Green to Amber or Red status
recalculates cost of delay with updated figures
runs updated sensitivity analysis reflecting the new cost reality
evaluates whether the investment recommendation changes under the revised numbers
suggests scope reduction or phased delivery options if NPV turns negative or marginal
flags the 40% variance as a risk indicator for remaining cost estimates
